metadata.name: Mutate — Gemrazer cast for mutate onto Canopy Crawler; merge verified, trigger targets and destroys opponent's artifact
metadata.description: P1 controls Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) and Arcane Signet on the battlefield. P2 controls a second Arcane Signet. P1 holds Gemrazer (4/4 Beast, Mutate {1}{G}{G}, Reach, Trample, Whenever this creature mutates destroy target artifact or enchantment an opponent controls). During P1's precombat main phase, P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost ({1}{G}{G}), targeting Canopy Crawler (non-Human creature P1 owns — CR 702.140a). The script verifies: (1) Gemrazer on stack as MutatingCreatureSpell (count 1), (2) after merge resolves — Canopy Crawler still on battlefield as the merged permanent, trigger on stack (count 1 from Whenever this creature mutates) with its declared target auto-selected as P2's Arcane Signet (the only legal opponent-controlled artifact/enchantment — CR 601.2c/603.3d, PB-EF3 EF-W-MISS-10), (3) after trigger resolves — stack empty, Gemrazer (merged permanent) still on P1 battlefield, P1's own Arcane Signet survives (not a legal target — controller filter), P2's Arcane Signet is destroyed. The script also confirms corner case #32 (Mutate Stack Ordering): Gemrazer on top (mutate_on_top: true) is a superset of the over/under choice mechanics.
metadata.generation_notes: Gemrazer card definition at crates/card-defs/src/defs/gemrazer.rs: 4/4 Beast, ManaCost {generic:3, green:1}, Mutate {1}{G}{G}, Reach, Trample, AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: TriggerCondition::WhenMutates, effect: Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }, targets: vec![TargetRequirement::TargetPermanentWithFilter(TargetFilter { has_card_types: [Artifact, Enchantment], controller: Opponent, .. })] }. Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) is a legal mutate target: on battlefield, creature, non-Human, owned by P1 (CR 702.140a). P1's own Arcane Signet is present as a decoy — it does NOT match the trigger's Opponent controller filter, so it is never a candidate. P2's Arcane Signet is the sole legal target and is auto-selected (CR 603.3d deterministic first-match) and destroyed. Mana pool pre-loaded with 1 colorless + 2 green to pay the mutate cost {1}{G}{G} (generic maps to colorless in the engine). The cast_spell_mutate harness action (replay_harness.rs) uses: card_name=card field, target_creature=target_creature field, mutate_on_top field. The mutate trigger (TriggerCondition::WhenMutates) is converted to a runtime TriggeredAbilityDef in enrich_spec_from_def (replay_harness.rs), which fires SelfMutates on GameEvent::CreatureMutated, carrying its declared targets (PB-EF3 A1). At resolution, flush_pending_triggers auto-selects the sole legal opponent artifact via the CR 603.3d target-selection loop (PB-EF3 A2), so Effect::DestroyPermanent resolves against P2's Arcane Signet. Corner case #32 (Mutate Stack Ordering): verified by over/under choice (mutate_on_top: true) and assertion that merged permanent (Canopy Crawler object) remains on battlefield.

disputes.description: Gemrazer's 'Whenever this creature mutates' trigger uses Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }. Prior to PB-EF3 (EF-W-MISS-10), when the trigger was placed on the stack by flush_pending_triggers, the stack object's targets vec was empty regardless of the CardDef's declared targets — the enrich path hardcoded `targets: vec![]` and a registry-fallback bug additionally raw-indexed the wrong ability on multi-ability cards. PB-EF3 fixed both: enrich now forwards the CardDef's declared `targets`, and CR 603.3d auto-target selection picks the sole legal opponent-controlled artifact/enchantment deterministically. The script now exercises the corrected behavior: P2's Arcane Signet is destroyed; P1's own Arcane Signet survives because it fails the controller filter.
disputes.resolution: Fixed by PB-EF3 (scutemob-103, EF-W-MISS-10): replay_harness.rs enrich forwards CardDef `targets` for all AbilityDefinition::Triggered conversions; abilities.rs auto-target selection treats the runtime triggered_abilities vec as authoritative for PendingTriggerKind::Normal. Script updated to add P2's Arcane Signet as the legal target and assert its destruction.

disputes.description: Gemrazer's mutate trigger targets 'artifact or enchantment an opponent controls' — modeled as TargetPermanentWithFilter(TargetFilter { has_card_types: [Artifact, Enchantment], controller: Opponent }), which is a faithful (not approximate) rendering of the oracle text; no DSL gap remains for this card.
disputes.resolution: Not a gap — TargetFilter's has_card_types + controller fields express the oracle text exactly.

script.step_note: P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost {1}{G}{G}, targeting Canopy Crawler (non-Human Beast P1 owns). Gemrazer goes onto the stack as a MutatingCreatureSpell. After both players pass priority, the spell resolves: Gemrazer merges with Canopy Crawler (Gemrazer on top, CR 702.140c). The merge fires a 'whenever this creature mutates' trigger (CR 702.140d), whose declared target (an artifact or enchantment an opponent controls) is auto-selected as P2's Arcane Signet — the only legal candidate. The trigger goes on the stack. Both players pass again; the trigger resolves and destroys P2's Arcane Signet. Stack empties.

actions.description: Initial: Canopy Crawler (2/2 Beast) and Arcane Signet on P1 battlefield. P2 controls its own Arcane Signet. Gemrazer in P1 hand. Stack empty. Mana pool loaded with {1}{C}{G}{G} for mutate cost.
actions.note: Turn 4 so Canopy Crawler has no summoning sickness. P1 mana pool contains 1 colorless + 2 green (= {1}{G}{G} = mutate cost) pre-loaded. P1's own Arcane Signet is a decoy (fails the trigger's Opponent controller filter); P2's Arcane Signet is the sole legal target for Gemrazer's mutate trigger.

CR 702.140a: P1 casts Gemrazer using its mutate cost {1}{G}{G} (alternative cost). Target is Canopy Crawler — a non-Human creature P1 owns on the battlefield. mutate_on_top: true means Gemrazer's card is placed on top of the merged permanent. The harness cast_spell_mutate action: card_name='Gemrazer', target_creature='Canopy Crawler', mutate_on_top=true. Translates to Command::CastSpell with alt_cost=Mutate, mutate_target=canopy_crawler_id, mutate_on_top=true.

actions.description: After casting: Gemrazer is on the stack (count 1). Canopy Crawler and Arcane Signet remain on P1 battlefield (merge has not resolved). Gemrazer no longer in P1 hand.
actions.note: CR 601.2: Spell placed on stack when cast. Gemrazer moves from P1 hand to stack as a MutatingCreatureSpell (StackObjectKind::MutatingCreatureSpell). Canopy Crawler remains on the battlefield — it is the merge target, not yet affected. Stack count = 1.
CR 116.3: P1 passes, P2 passes. Stack top resolves (Gemrazer as MutatingCreatureSpell). CR 405.5: LIFO.

resolution.note: CR 702.140c: Mutate target (Canopy Crawler) is still legal. Gemrazer merges with Canopy Crawler: Gemrazer's component data is prepended to merged_components (index 0, mutate_on_top=true). CR 729.2a: Topmost component (Gemrazer) supplies base characteristics. CR 702.140e: ALL components contribute abilities. CR 729.2c: No ETB trigger — same object. GameEvent::CreatureMutated emitted. Gemrazer's card object removed from state.objects (CR 729.2b).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Mutating creature spell resolves via resolution.rs StackObjectKind::MutatingCreatureSpell arm. After merge, GameEvent::CreatureMutated fires (abilities.rs check_triggers → SelfMutates → PendingTrigger pushed). flush_pending_triggers auto-selects the sole legal target (P2's Arcane Signet, CR 603.3d) and places the 'Whenever this creature mutates' trigger on the stack as StackObjectKind::TriggeredAbility.

actions.description: After merge resolves: one object on stack (the WhenMutates trigger, targeting P2's Arcane Signet). Gemrazer on P1 battlefield as the merged permanent (name from topmost component, CR 729.2a). Both Arcane Signets still on battlefield.
actions.note: CR 729.2a: The merged permanent's name (and all other characteristics) come from the topmost component, which is Gemrazer (mutate_on_top=true). The engine represents the merged permanent with the Canopy Crawler ObjectId (same object, CR 729.2c) but the card name displayed is 'Gemrazer' from the topmost component's characteristics. Stack count = 1 (the mutate trigger, targeting P2's Arcane Signet per CR 603.3d auto-selection). Canopy Crawler's name is no longer the active name — Gemrazer is.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority → top of stack (mutate trigger) resolves. CR 405.5: LIFO.

resolution.note: The trigger resolves. Effect::DestroyPermanent { target: DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } } looks up ctx.targets[0], which was set at trigger-queue time (PB-EF3 A2 / CR 603.3d) to P2's Arcane Signet — the sole permanent matching the trigger's Opponent-controlled artifact/enchantment filter. P2's Arcane Signet is destroyed and moves to P2's graveyard. P1's own Arcane Signet is untouched (never a candidate — fails the controller filter).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Triggered ability resolves. Effect::DestroyPermanent destroys P2's Arcane Signet (the auto-selected target). Stack empties.

actions.description: Final state: stack empty. Gemrazer (merged permanent) on P1 battlefield with its own Arcane Signet. P2's Arcane Signet destroyed (in P2 graveyard, not on battlefield). Life totals unchanged at 40/40.
actions.note: CR 729.2a: The merged permanent shows as 'Gemrazer' (topmost component's name). CR 729.2c: Same object as the original Canopy Crawler permanent. P1's own Arcane Signet survives (not a legal target — controller filter). P2's Arcane Signet is destroyed (PB-EF3 EF-W-MISS-10 fix) and moves to P2's graveyard (CR 704.5g / 700.4). Life totals unchanged: no damage dealt.
